skill.name: rust-pro
skill.description: Master Rust 1.75+ with modern async patterns, advanced type system features, and production-ready systems programming. Expert in the latest Rust ecosystem including Tokio, axum, and cutting-edge crates.
skill.summary: Expert Rust developer skill for modern Rust 1.75+, async programming, systems-level performance, and production applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 potential security issues. This is a Rust developer persona definition that provides guidance on Rust programming without any network, filesystem, or system access capabilities. Risk score: 0/100.

content.value_statement: Build high-performance, memory-safe Rust applications with expert guidance on async patterns, advanced type system features, and production-ready systems programming.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement async web services with Tokio runtime
Solve ownership, lifetime, and borrow checker issues
Implement safe FFI wrappers around C libraries
Optimize memory usage and cache locality
Create custom error handling with thiserror and anyhow
limitations: Cannot execute or compile Rust code directly
Cannot access local file system or project files
Cannot run benchmarks or profiling tools
Provides guidance only, not runtime execution

use_cases.title: Building Async Web Services
use_cases.description: Design and implement high-performance web APIs using axum, Tokio, and async patterns with proper error handling and backpressure

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Analyze and optimize Rust code for memory usage, cache efficiency, and zero-cost abstractions while maintaining safety

use_cases.title: FFI and Unsafe Code
use_cases.description: Create safe wrappers around C libraries with proper safety invariants and documentation for unsafe code blocks
use_cases.target_user: Developers integrating Rust with native libraries

prompt_templates.title: Basic Rust Question
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how [Rust concept] works in Rust 1.75+ and provide a code example.
prompt_templates.scenario: Learning a specific Rust feature or syntax

prompt_templates.title: Debug Ownership Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: I have this Rust code that fails with a borrow checker error. Analyze the ownership model and suggest a fix: [code]

prompt_templates.title: Design Async Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an async web service in Rust that handles [use case] with Tokio. Include error handling, proper shutdown, and testing approach.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Profile and optimize this Rust code for [metric]. Suggest zero-cost abstractions, memory layout improvements, or algorithm changes: [code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving runtime performance of Rust applications

output_examples.input: Explain async/await in Rust and show how to use Tokio for a concurrent web server
output_examples.output: Async/await in Rust provides a way to write asynchronous code that looks synchronous. With Tokio, you can build concurrent servers efficiently. Here's a basic example of an async HTTP server...

output_examples.input: Help me understand ownership model for the this code that manages a database connection pool
output_examples.output: For a database connection pool, you typically want multiple threads to access connections. This requires Arc<Mutex<ConnectionPool>> or a specialized pool like r2d2. Let me explain the ownership semantics...

best_practices: Prefer Result types over exceptions for error handling
Use Arc and Rc only when shared ownership is truly needed
Document all unsafe code blocks with explicit safety invariants
anti_patterns: Using unwrap() in production code without proper error handling
Ignoring the borrow checker by using unsafe instead of finding a safe solution
Blocking async code with synchronous I/O operations

faq.question: What Rust version does this skill cover?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Rust 1.75+ features including const generics, improved type inference, and async improvements.

faq.question: Can this skill write and compile code for me?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance, examples, and code suggestions but cannot execute or compile Rust code directly.

faq.question: Does this skill support Tokio and async programming?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill has extensive knowledge of Tokio ecosystem including axum, tower, hyper, and async patterns.

faq.question: Can this help with FFI and unsafe code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill covers safe abstractions over unsafe code, FFI patterns, and proper safety documentation.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: It covers unit tests, property-based testing with proptest, benchmarking with criterion.rs, and mocking with mockall.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for intermediate to advanced Rust developers. Beginners should start with basic Rust tutorials first.
